body:
![medium](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/medium-priority.svg)

The length check on the following line (`RAY_CHECK_LE(name.size(), PSEMNAMLEN)`) should be updated to check the length of the final semaphore name (`ret.size()`). Prepending `"/obj"` adds 4 characters to the length. On platforms with strict limits like macOS (where `PSEMNAMLEN` is typically 30), if `name.size()` is close to the limit, the resulting string will exceed it, causing `sem_open` to fail and the process to crash at the subsequent `RAY_CHECK_NE`.

![medium](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/medium-priority.svg)

Similar to `GetSemaphoreObjectName`, the length check on the following line should verify `ret.size()` instead of `name.size()`. The `"/hdr"` prefix increases the total length by 4 characters, which may exceed `PSEMNAMLEN` even if the input `name` is within the limit.

### `sem_getvalue` unsupported on macOS breaks new test **Medium Severity** `TestCrossNodeSemaphoreCreation` calls `sem_getvalue()` on lines 696–697, but this function is not implemented on macOS — it returns `-1` with `errno = ENOSYS`. Since the test compiles under `#if defined(__APPLE__) || defined(__linux__)`, the `ASSERT_EQ(0, sem_getvalue(...))` assertions will always fail on macOS, causing a test failure on that platform.
 
Reviewed by [Cursor Bugbot](https://cursor.com/bugbot) for commit 92725a21d63770f50b543cdcf11dc95cb9eb5097. Configure [here](https://www.cursor.com/dashboard/bugbot).

### Tightened size check crashes for valid semaphore names **High Severity** The new `RAY_CHECK_LE(ret.size(), PSEMNAMLEN)` check validates the full prefixed name (`/obj` + name or `/hdr` + name) against `PSEMNAMLEN` (30), but `Init()` still allows `name` to be up to 30 bytes. Since the prefix adds 4 bytes, any `name` longer than 26 bytes will crash here. On 64-bit Linux where `pid_max` defaults to 4194304, a 7-digit PID produces a 27-byte name, making `ret` 31 bytes and triggering the CHECK failure. The old code checked `name.size()` instead of `ret.size()`, so this is a regression for previously valid names.
Additional Locations (1) - [`src/ray/core_worker/experimental_mutable_object_manager.cc#L50-L51`](https://github.com/ray-project/ray/blob/4792c0ff2a68dc82328acc639976464721ce7fee/src/ray/core_worker/experimental_mutable_object_manager.cc#L50-L51)
 
Reviewed by [Cursor Bugbot](https://cursor.com/bugbot) for commit 4792c0ff2a68dc82328acc639976464721ce7fee. Configure [here](https://www.cursor.com/dashboard/bugbot).

Stale — this review was on an earlier commit. Current HEAD (`a816079028`) wraps both `sem_getvalue` assertions in `#ifdef __linux__` after Gemini flagged the same issue. The `RegisterChannel` success assertion still runs on both platforms.

Valid — confirmed and fixed in `a816079028`. Reverted the check back to `RAY_CHECK_LE(name.size(), PSEMNAMLEN)` to avoid regressing Linux with 7-digit PIDs (pid_max=4194304 can produce a 27-byte name, making `/obj` + name = 31 bytes trip the tighter check even though Linux sem_open accepts much longer names). `Init()` already caps `name.size()`, and `sem_open` returning `SEM_FAILED` on oversized macOS names is caught by the existing `RAY_CHECK_NE(..., SEM_FAILED)` downstream, so the looser bound is still safe. Thanks for catching!
